what_it_does: This file defines the integration contract for DNA's decision-card render: a consolidated checklist of exactly what the card must emit and do to plug into the live host machinery, the silent failure for each missed seam, projection-verified statuses, and a ready prove-on-one harness.
observations: It enumerates seven required seams: render from resolved decision, mount through host, emit decision:rendered, stamp and wire the take control, host the automatic in-card Ask, refresh after a take, and display write errors on the card.
It insists on BARE CANONICAL decision://<frame>/<id> addresses everywhere and warns that #elem-style addresses break host open and take dispatch.
It notes that the host is variant-agnostic and that the Ask binds automatically once the card emits decision:rendered with the right element and address.
It flags the write-error display seam as the card's responsibility because the card overlay sits above surface chrome (z-60 vs z-50/z-45).
It provides a ready harness at surface/app/scripts/prove-decision-harness.js that asserts the full loop and auto-detects real vs synthetic cards.
It references a companion document DECISION-SURFACE-BUILD.md for vision/build-order.
standouts: The strict canonical-address requirement, including wildcard's loud refusal of non-canonical addresses (TEST9), turns an old silent 'decided reads pending' failure into a scream-at-emit failure.
The write-error display is explicitly called out as the one seam projection cannot cover because the card overlay outranks surface chrome.
The prove-decision harness is designed to run the same second the card renders, with a safe no-take mode for real decisions and a full-loop mode only for throwaways.
conventions: Uses a numbered seam/checklist format with per-item verified status and silent-failure warnings.
Labels external verification as 'projection-verified' and hazards with warning emoji.
Refers to specific API paths, window globals, DOM IDs, and z-index values as concrete contract terms.
Keeps the file local and non-normative about overall architecture ranking.
concerns: The contract leans heavily on 'projection-verified' claims without showing the verification data in the file itself.
It assumes a brittle z-index layering (card z-60 above Notice z-50 and V z-45) without explaining how that ordering is maintained.
The harness can flip real state if an operator passes decide on a non-throwaway decision, creating a footgun.
No code symbols are present, so the contract's claims are not anchored to executable definitions in this file.
notes: This is the host-integration contract for DNA's decision card, spelling out the exact render/mount/emit/take/ask/refresh/write-error seams and a same-second prove harness.
questions: What exactly are DNA, fork, projection, and wildcard in this codebase?
How is the uniqueness/enforcement of option LABEL as decided_value guaranteed?
How does an in-place refresh supersede the host's full re-mount in practice?
What happens if multiple decision cards render simultaneously?
Where is the registry definition of subtype and render_kind maintained?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a consolidated checklist of exactly what the card must emit/do to plug into live machinery, and the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: It is trying to make the decision-card integration succeed in one pass by making every host seam explicit, attaching a concrete silent-failure consequence to each, and supplying an immediate runnable harness so the loop can be verified the moment the card paints.
novelty: It combines a seam-level integration contract with per-seam silent-failure warnings and a live browser harness that auto-distinguishes a real mounted card from a synthetic stub.
contribution: It contributes a precise, seam-level integration contract plus a runnable prove-decision harness that a fused system could keep to verify decision-card/host wiring without ambiguity.
summary_for_embedding: A markdown host-integration contract for DNA's decision card that lists seven required seams, their silent-failure modes, projection-verified statuses, and a browser prove-decision harness for one-pass verification.
intention_for_embedding: To specify exactly how DNA's decision card must render, mount, emit events, handle takes, and display write errors so it plugs into the host machinery without silent failures.
